## [00:00 - 03:02]

All right guys, so let's talk about funnels within the coaching and online course space. These are really, really different funnels to what you would see in e-commerce or in the lead gen space. So if you're working with mortgage brokers or dentists, for example, fundamentally different kettle of fish, these funnels are a lot more complicated to essentially crack or validate if you will. And so it's really important that especially if you're working with a brand new coach or someone that's never been successful with Facebook ads, that you do apply the strategy that we teach you in here, which is the minimum viable funnel using rapid fire testing in order to validate the audiences, the offers and the messaging. Now how do you do that? All right. Before we go into what all of that is, let's have a look at actual funnels that you will end up with, right? So this is typically the funnels that you'll be working with when you're working with these kinds of clients. So before you even start to build stuff for your clients, and these questions should really be established as well in the client onboarding process. So on the sales call when you're onboarding the lead, so you really get a clear idea. On what kind of margins or figures they're working with. So first one is figuring out what is the lifetime value of their customer. And that will help you to assess what the acceptable cost per lead is or cost per acquisition. So how much are they willing to spend? The reason I say this is that for example, a lot of coaches are selling membership programs. So a monthly recurring program, maybe it sells at $400 a month. And so your acceptable cost per acquisition may in fact be $800 if they are getting a 20% churn rate, which means that the average customer stays with them for five months. So that means they make money even if it costs them twice as much as what it does for the first month of membership. Likewise, you might have a coach or a consultant that's selling a high ticket program. Maybe they're selling something for $5,000, for example. And then on the back of that, they upsell them into a mastermind worth $50,000. And so the actual lifetime value of that customer may be worth $20,000 or $25,000. Meaning that your acceptable cost per lead can go up to $200 or $300 depending on how much they're closing. So use the calculator, but establish that lifetime value and really figure out what is the acceptable cost per lead. Don't let them tell you this because they're always going to tell you it's $5 or $10.

## [03:02 - 06:07]

You need to set the right expectations with them. All right. So the most basic form of funnel that you can possibly work with, I like, you can actually just make it the add to the lead magnet. But this is kind of like, you know, if you want to have some kind of reputation in this space, this is probably the most basic level funnel. So you're sending essentially sending people to a lead magnet. Now remember a lead magnet is a magnet to attract leads makes sense, right? Since there's a juicy offer to bring leads through the door to bring authority, affinity and awareness. You cannot just go straight to a sales page when you're working with these kinds of funnels. So you cannot just target a cold audience and send them straight to a sales page. Those will not work, those ads. Okay. It's very rare that those work. So what we want to do is we want to run ads to a lead magnet and then retarget anyone that opted in and send them to the sales page and the offer. So you can see here, this is really basic because it's actually missing the retargeting step where people that visited that offer. And that's kind of what you're looking at here. This is the typical funnel length that you're going to be working with when you're working with this type of client. And then if it turns out that, for example, your top of funnel. In this funnel, for example, you've got an ad going to a lead magnet, people are opting in, you're then retargeting the people that opted in and sending them to a sales page. And then anyone that visited that sales page or offer, you're then retargeting with that offer again. So this is typically what will work. This works for high ticket and low ticket as well. Now if you're finding that your top of funnel, your ad to your lead magnet is costing you too much money or it's not really working, typically what a lot of people will do here is they'll extend the funnel at the beginning, at the front end. So they'll add a video here or some kind of piece of content and then they'll retarget people that viewed this or engaged with this with the lead magnet offer and then so on and so on.
